title: Dog Breed Classifier
text:
Try out my dog breed classifier: Dog Breed Classifier
This was my second attempt to build a Fastai model. Although the second lesson in the Fastai course is also a dog breed classifier, this code did not follow that example. I trained a model using the Stanford Dogs Dataset: http://vision.stanford.edu/aditya86/ImageNetDogs/
This exercise was to understand the components required to train a Fastai model as well as testing out the various pretrained vision models in the timm library. This model currently uses the levit-384 model

title: About
text:
Hi!
I’m Nicholas Huang and I’m a passionate data scientist.
I’ve studied a Bachelor of Science majoring in Data Science at the University of Melbourne. I’ve been working as a data analyst for 3 years now. 1.5 years at Infosys and currently 1.5 years at Roy Morgan.
I always been amazed by how Data Science can be utilised to make predictions and classifications about the world. Being away from Data Science in the last few years has made me rediscover my passion for the field. This blog is to document my data science learning journey.

title: Dog and Cat Classifier
text:
Try out my dog vs cat classifier: Dog and cat classifier
This project was inspired by the first lesson in the Fastai course. This was my first introduction to the Fastai library, Gradio and Hugging Faces.
It was definitely intimidating and frustrating learning about these new resources as well as trying to relearn git and linux for my own sake. However, it was very satisfying to get it all working.
It is amazing that this was the result of very few lines of code.
The process to creating this definitely took longer than expected and showed me I have a lot to learn when it comes to data science and programming in general but I’m happy to have gotten started!
